transcript.text: Hello, I just got an idea popping in my head, and it's popped in my head a few times, so I figured I'd do a video now, since it's in my head, and it was chaos versus order.

And, so, things grow in order, like this.
So, what's the purpose of the chaos?
Um, I think chaos is the cutting edge of order.
It's the surface of order.
It's where, it's the connection point between order and the rest of the universe.

So, and that's where the chaos is, it's kind of, like, something growing, and then it's collecting all of this stuff out of the cosmos, and bringing it to it, you know, and as it's collecting it, all the stuff is all chaotic.

So, um, so, like, examples of, like, chaos would be, like, somebody, um, some kind of crazy person, um, you know, somebody losing their mind and talking to themselves all the time, or something like this.
Because, this would be a person who's, like, exploring a part of themselves that they've never explored before, going off in some new direction.
